document: γ-Glycine γ-Glycine γ-Glycine γ-Glycine γ-Glycine 1169370 GLYCIN18 Gamma Glycine PMC-001 γ-Glycine γ-phase glycine 1169370 GLYCIN18 Gamma Glycine PMC-001 γ-Glycine γ-phase glycine 1169370 GLYCIN18 Gamma Glycine PMC-001 γ-Glycine γ-phase glycine PMC-001 C2H5NO2 GLYCIN18 1169370 P3_1 Trigonal 10.1088/1402-4896/ace861  PMC-001 γ-Glycine C2H5NO2 Organic Molecular Crystal P3_1 Trigonal 10.1088/1402-4896/ace861 1169370 GLYCIN18 Gamma Glycine PMC-001 γ-Glycine γ-phase glycine piezoelectric ferroelectric Gamma-glycine (γ-glycine), chemical formula C2H5NO2, molecular weight 75.07, is a single-component organic molecular crystal. It crystallizes in the non-centrosymmetric, polar trigonal space group P3_1 (No. 144), with unit cell a = b = 7.037 Å, c = 5.478 Å and γ = 120°, Z = 3. The structure is built from hydrogen-bonded glycine zwitterions. The crystal is piezoelectric: piezoresponse force microscopy (PFM) on the as-grown (100) face measured an effective shear (in-plane, d15) coefficient of about 1 pm/V and an effective longitudinal (out-of-plane, d33) coefficient of about 3 pm/V. Local mechanical polishing enhanced these roughly five-fold and three-fold, to about 5 and 10 pm/V respectively, and switching-spectroscopy PFM (SS-PFM) revealed local ferroelectric switching in the polished regions with a coercive field of about 3 GV/m. Gamma-glycine is biocompatible. The crystal structure corresponds to CSD refcode GLYCIN18 (CCDC 1169370), determined by single-crystal X-ray diffraction at 295 K with an R factor of 2.29% and a density of 1.592 g/cm3. The piezoelectric and local ferroelectric characterization was reported by Ivanov et al. in Physica Scripta (2023).

β-Glycine β-Glycine β-Glycine β-Glycine β-Glycine Beta Glycine PMC-002 β-Glycine β-phase glycine Beta Glycine PMC-002 β-Glycine β-phase glycine Beta Glycine PMC-002 β-Glycine β-phase glycine PMC-002 C2H5NO2   P2_1 Monoclinic 10.1016/j.nanoen.2026.112075 10.1039/d1cc02608a PMC-002 β-Glycine C2H5NO2 Organic Molecular Crystal P2_1 Monoclinic 10.1016/j.nanoen.2026.112075 10.1039/d1cc02608a Beta Glycine PMC-002 β-Glycine β-phase glycine piezoelectric ferroelectric Beta-glycine (β-glycine), chemical formula C2H5NO2, molecular weight 75.07, is a single-component organic molecular crystal and a metastable polymorph of glycine. It crystallizes in the non-centrosymmetric, polar monoclinic space group P2_1 (No. 4), with unit cell a = 5.0757 Å, b = 6.1737 Å, c = 5.3882 Å and β = 113.422°, Z = 2. The structure is built from hydrogen-bonded glycine zwitterions. Beta-glycine is piezoelectric and is known for a giant shear piezoelectric coefficient (d16) of about 178 pm/V. In this work, dual-AC-resonance-tracking piezoresponse force microscopy (PFM) measured an effective piezoelectric coefficient of about 9.9 pm/V for the beta-glycine crystal and 10.3 pm/V for a flexible beta-glycine/polycaprolactone (PCL) composite film, and ferroelectric hysteresis-loop measurements demonstrated ferroelectric switching. The composite film was used to build a biocompatible piezoelectric nanogenerator (PENG) that produced electrical output under mechanical pressure and ultrasound. The crystal structure (Crystallography Open Database entry 7128793) was determined by single-crystal X-ray diffraction at 100 K with an R factor of 2.29%, a density of 1.609 g/cm3, a colorless needle habit, and was reported by Hoser et al. in Chemical Communications (2021). The piezoelectric and nanogenerator characterization was reported by Bai et al. in Nano Energy (2026).

Croconic Acid Croconic Acid Croconic Acid Croconic Acid Croconic Acid 147324 4,5-dihydroxy-4-cyclopentene-1,2,3-trione Croconic Acid H2C5O5 PMC-003 147324 4,5-dihydroxy-4-cyclopentene-1,2,3-trione Croconic Acid H2C5O5 PMC-003 147324 4,5-dihydroxy-4-cyclopentene-1,2,3-trione Croconic Acid H2C5O5 PMC-003 PMC-003 C5H2O5  147324 Pca2_1 Orthorhombic 10.1063/1.4813500 10.1039/b100020i PMC-003 Croconic Acid C5H2O5 Organic Molecular Crystal Pca2_1 Orthorhombic 10.1063/1.4813500 10.1039/b100020i 147324 4,5-dihydroxy-4-cyclopentene-1,2,3-trione Croconic Acid H2C5O5 PMC-003 piezoelectric ferroelectric Croconic acid (4,5-dihydroxy-4-cyclopentene-1,2,3-trione), chemical formula C5H2O5, molecular weight 142.07, is a single-component organic molecular crystal and a prototypical proton-transfer-type organic ferroelectric. It crystallizes in the non-centrosymmetric, polar orthorhombic space group Pca2_1 (No. 29), with unit cell a = 8.7108 Å, b = 5.1683 Å, c = 10.9562 Å, Z = 4. Molecules are linked into chains by resonance-assisted O-H...O hydrogen bonds. Croconic acid shows a large room-temperature spontaneous polarization (about 21 microC/cm2 experimentally) arising from intermolecular proton transfer and charge redistribution along the hydrogen bonds. In this work, first-principles density functional theory (DFT, GGA) with the Berry-phase method computed a spontaneous polarization of about 22.3-24.7 microC/cm2 along the c axis, and a one-dimensional Hamiltonian with Monte Carlo simulation predicted a Curie temperature of about 756 K, with polarization stable to roughly 400 K. The crystal structure (CCDC 147324) was determined by single-crystal X-ray diffraction at 293 K with an R factor of 5.49%, a density of 1.913 g/cm3, and a yellow hexagonal-prism habit. The ferroelectric-mechanism study was reported by Cai et al. in The Journal of Chemical Physics (2013).

2-Methylbenzimidazole 2-Methylbenzimidazole 2-Methylbenzimidazole 2-Methylbenzimidazole 2-Methylbenzimidazole 2-Methylbenzimidazole 2-methyl-1H-benzimidazole 909441 KOWYEA02 MBI PMC-004 2-Methylbenzimidazole 2-methyl-1H-benzimidazole 909441 KOWYEA02 MBI PMC-004 2-Methylbenzimidazole 2-methyl-1H-benzimidazole 909441 KOWYEA02 MBI PMC-004 PMC-004 C8H8N2 KOWYEA02 909441 Pn Monoclinic   PMC-004 2-Methylbenzimidazole C8H8N2 Organic Molecular Crystal Pn Monoclinic 2-Methylbenzimidazole 2-methyl-1H-benzimidazole 909441 KOWYEA02 MBI PMC-004 piezoelectric ferroelectric 2-Methylbenzimidazole (MBI), chemical formula C8H8N2, molecular weight 132.16, is a single-component organic molecular crystal and a proton-transfer-type biaxial ferroelectric that remains ferroelectric above room temperature. It crystallizes in the non-centrosymmetric, polar monoclinic space group Pn (No. 7) with a pseudo-tetragonal metric, unit cell a = 13.9639 Å, b = 7.2107 Å, c = 13.9639 Å, Z = 8. MBI molecules lie flat in the a-b plane and form hydrogen-bonded chains, with two polar axes along the [110] and [1-10] directions and van der Waals contacts linking the chains. MBI single crystals have a remanent polarization of about 5.2 microC/cm2 and a low coercive field (~11 kV/cm). In this work, micron-thick MBI films were grown by low-temperature physical vapor deposition (~230 K) followed by restrained crystallization on warming; crystallization near 287 K produced ferroelectric films with remanent polarization up to about 2.5-2.6 microC/cm2 and an effective coercive field of about 87 kV/cm, with the [110] polar axis aligned out-of-plane. Ferroelectric switching was confirmed by polarization-electric-field hysteresis loops measured in situ. The crystal structure (CCDC 909441) was determined by single-crystal synchrotron X-ray diffraction at 300 K with an R factor of 5.11%, a density of 1.249 g/cm3, and a colorless plate habit. The film-fabrication study was reported by Yuan et al.

2-Phenylmalondialdehyde 2-Phenylmalondialdehyde 2-Phenylmalondialdehyde 2-Phenylmalondialdehyde 2-Phenylmalondialdehyde 1238105 2-Phenylmalondialdehyde PMC-005 PROLON PhMDA Phenylmalondialdehyde 1238105 2-Phenylmalondialdehyde PMC-005 PROLON PhMDA Phenylmalondialdehyde 1238105 2-Phenylmalondialdehyde PMC-005 PROLON PhMDA Phenylmalondialdehyde PMC-005 C9H8O2 PROLON 1238105 Pna2_1 Orthorhombic 10.7567/JJAP.53.09PD07  PMC-005 2-Phenylmalondialdehyde C9H8O2 Organic Molecular Crystal Pna2_1 Orthorhombic 10.7567/JJAP.53.09PD07 1238105 2-Phenylmalondialdehyde PMC-005 PROLON PhMDA Phenylmalondialdehyde piezoelectric ferroelectric 2-Phenylmalondialdehyde (PhMDA), chemical formula C9H8O2, molecular weight 148.16, is a single-component hydrogen-bonded organic molecular crystal and a room-temperature ferroelectric. It crystallizes in the non-centrosymmetric, polar orthorhombic space group Pna2_1 (No. 33), with unit cell a = 7.523 Å, b = 17.165 Å, c = 5.552 Å, Z = 4. Molecules carrying the beta-diketone enol O=C-C=C-OH group are connected by O-H...O hydrogen bonds along the [102] and [1 0 -2] directions; asymmetric off-center proton configurations break inversion symmetry and give a spontaneous polarization of about 9 microC/cm2 along the c axis, with a Curie temperature above 363 K (values from earlier ferroelectric studies). In this work, room-temperature terahertz radiation from PhMDA single crystals was generated by femtosecond-laser optical rectification, a second-order nonlinear optical effect; the emitted single-cycle terahertz pulse had an amplitude comparable to that of a ZnTe reference emitter, attributed to a long coherence length of about 130-800 microns. No piezoelectric coefficient was measured. The crystal structure (CCDC 1238105, CSD refcode PROLON) was determined by single-crystal X-ray diffraction at 111 K with an R factor of 3.2% and a density of 1.373 g/cm3. The terahertz-radiation study was reported by Guan et al. in the Japanese Journal of Applied Physics (2014).

5,6-Dichloro-2-methylbenzimidazole 5,6-Dichloro-2-methylbenzimidazole 5,6-Dichloro-2-methylbenzimidazole 5,6-Dichloro-2-methylbenzimidazole 5,6-Dichloro-2-methylbenzimidazole 5,6-Dichloro-2-methylbenzimidazole 5,6-dichloro-2-methyl-1H-benzimidazole 909439 DC-MBI PMC-006 REZBOP 5,6-Dichloro-2-methylbenzimidazole 5,6-dichloro-2-methyl-1H-benzimidazole 909439 DC-MBI PMC-006 REZBOP 5,6-Dichloro-2-methylbenzimidazole 5,6-dichloro-2-methyl-1H-benzimidazole 909439 DC-MBI PMC-006 REZBOP PMC-006 C8H6Cl2N2 REZBOP 909439 Pca2_1 Orthorhombic 10.1021/jacs.8b08591  PMC-006 5,6-Dichloro-2-methylbenzimidazole C8H6Cl2N2 Organic Molecular Crystal Pca2_1 Orthorhombic 10.1021/jacs.8b08591 5,6-Dichloro-2-methylbenzimidazole 5,6-dichloro-2-methyl-1H-benzimidazole 909439 DC-MBI PMC-006 REZBOP piezoelectric ferroelectric 5,6-Dichloro-2-methylbenzimidazole (DC-MBI), chemical formula C8H6Cl2N2, molecular weight 201.07, is a single-component hydrogen-bonded organic molecular crystal and an above-room-temperature ferroelectric. At room temperature it crystallizes in the non-centrosymmetric, polar orthorhombic space group Pca2_1 (No. 29), with unit cell a = 14.238 Å, b = 5.6866 Å, c = 10.398 Å, Z = 4. DC-MBI molecules link through intermolecular N-H...N hydrogen bonds into chains stacked along the b axis, with the polarity of each chain directed along the c axis; ferroelectricity arises from positional ordering of the bridging protons. In this work, variable-temperature powder and single-crystal X-ray diffraction combined with symmetry-adapted distortion-mode analysis showed that DC-MBI undergoes an unusual first-order, symmetry-lowering transition on warming from orthorhombic Pca2_1 (T below about 400 K) to monoclinic, still-polar Pc; second-harmonic-generation measurements confirmed the crystal remains non-centrosymmetric up to sublimation. No piezoelectric coefficient was measured, and the high room-temperature coercive field prevented ferroelectric hysteresis switching except at elevated temperature. The crystal structure (CCDC 909439) was determined by single-crystal X-ray diffraction at 295 K with an R factor of 4.27%, a density of 1.586 g/cm3, and a colorless block habit. The phase-transition study was reported by Liu et al. in the Journal of the American Chemical Society (2018); the structure and ferroelectricity were originally reported by Horiuchi et al. in Nature Communications (2012).

L-Alanine L-Alanine L-Alanine L-Alanine L-Alanine (S)-Alanine 1203201 L-2-aminopropanoic acid L-Alanine LALNIN01 PMC-007 (S)-Alanine 1203201 L-2-aminopropanoic acid L-Alanine LALNIN01 PMC-007 (S)-Alanine 1203201 L-2-aminopropanoic acid L-Alanine LALNIN01 PMC-007 PMC-007 C3H7NO2 LALNIN01 1203201 P2_12_12_1 Orthorhombic 10.1016/j.msec.2019.110468  PMC-007 L-Alanine C3H7NO2 Organic Molecular Crystal P2_12_12_1 Orthorhombic 10.1016/j.msec.2019.110468 (S)-Alanine 1203201 L-2-aminopropanoic acid L-Alanine LALNIN01 PMC-007 piezoelectric L-Alanine, chemical formula C3H7NO2, molecular weight 89.09, is a single-component organic amino-acid molecular crystal. It crystallizes in the non-centrosymmetric, non-polar (enantiomorphic) orthorhombic space group P2_1 2_1 2_1 (No. 19, point group 222), with unit cell a = 6.032 Å, b = 12.343 Å, c = 5.784 Å, Z = 4. Zwitterionic alanine molecules are linked by a three-dimensional network of hydrogen bonds. Because the 222 point group is non-polar, L-alanine is piezoelectric but not pyroelectric or ferroelectric; its only non-zero piezoelectric coefficients (d14, d25, d36) are shear terms, so a piezoelectric response is activated solely by shear strain. In this work, L-alanine nanocrystals grown inside anodized-alumina nanopores with preferred [001] orientation produced a piezoelectric current that detected mechanical pressures as low as about 3 Pa at atmospheric ambient, with current amplitude increasing linearly with applied pressure; the sensitivity is attributed to easily deformed hydrogen bonds. No numerical piezoelectric coefficient was reported. The crystal structure (CCDC 1203201, CSD refcode LALNIN01) was determined by single-crystal X-ray diffraction at 295 K with an R factor of 6.1% and a density of 1.374 g/cm3. The piezoelectric pressure-sensing study, which also covered beta-glycine nanocrystals, was reported by Bishara et al. in Materials Science & Engineering C (2020).

meta-Nitroaniline meta-Nitroaniline meta-Nitroaniline meta-Nitroaniline meta-Nitroaniline 1212672 3-Nitroaniline MNIANL10 PMC-008 m-Nitroaniline mNA meta-Nitroaniline 1212672 3-Nitroaniline MNIANL10 PMC-008 m-Nitroaniline mNA meta-Nitroaniline 1212672 3-Nitroaniline MNIANL10 PMC-008 m-Nitroaniline mNA meta-Nitroaniline PMC-008 C6H6N2O2 MNIANL10 1212672 Pbc2_1 Orthorhombic 10.1103/PhysRevLett.81.5426 10.1039/P29730001197 PMC-008 meta-Nitroaniline C6H6N2O2 Organic Molecular Crystal Pbc2_1 Orthorhombic 10.1103/PhysRevLett.81.5426 10.1039/P29730001197 1212672 3-Nitroaniline MNIANL10 PMC-008 m-Nitroaniline mNA meta-Nitroaniline piezoelectric meta-Nitroaniline (mNA, 3-nitroaniline), chemical formula C6H6N2O2, molecular weight 138.12, is a single-component polar organic molecular crystal widely studied as a nonlinear-optical material. It crystallizes in the non-centrosymmetric, polar orthorhombic space group Pbc2_1 (No. 29, point group mm2), with unit cell a = 6.501 Å, b = 19.330 Å, c = 5.082 Å, Z = 4, and the polar axis along c. Because the mm2 point group is polar, mNA is piezoelectric and pyroelectric-compatible. In this work, three piezoelectric coefficients of mNA single crystals were measured at room temperature by synchrotron X-ray multiple diffraction, monitoring field-induced lattice strain via the converse piezoelectric effect: the transverse |d31| = 7.3 x 10^-11 m/V (about 73 pm/V), |d32| = 16.5 x 10^-11 m/V (about 165 pm/V, confirmed as 14.9 x 10^-11 m/V in a second reflection), and the longitudinal |d33| = 10.3 x 10^-11 m/V (about 103 pm/V); strain varied linearly and reversibly with applied field. The authors note these inverse-effect values run larger than direct-effect literature values and are presented as a feasibility demonstration rather than precise determinations. The crystal structure (CCDC 1212672, CSD refcode MNIANL10) was determined by single-crystal X-ray diffraction at 295 K with an R factor of 8.4% and a density of 1.437 g/cm3, reported by Skapski and Stevenson (1973). The piezoelectric-coefficient measurement was reported by Avanci et al. in Physical Review Letters (1998).

L-Histidine Hydrochloride Monohydrate L-Histidine Hydrochloride Monohydrate L-Histidine Hydrochloride Monohydrate L-Histidine Hydrochloride Monohydrate L-Histidine Hydrochloride Monohydrate 1176648 CAS 5934-29-2 HISTCM12 L-Histidine Hydrochloride Monohydrate L-Histidine monohydrochloride monohydrate L-Histidine·HCl·H₂O L-Histidinium chloride monohydrate PMC-009 1176648 CAS 5934-29-2 HISTCM12 L-Histidine Hydrochloride Monohydrate L-Histidine monohydrochloride monohydrate L-Histidine·HCl·H₂O L-Histidinium chloride monohydrate PMC-009 1176648 CAS 5934-29-2 HISTCM12 L-Histidine Hydrochloride Monohydrate L-Histidine monohydrochloride monohydrate L-Histidine·HCl·H₂O L-Histidinium chloride monohydrate PMC-009 PMC-009 C6H10N3O2+·Cl−·H2O HISTCM12 1176648 P2₁2₁2₁ orthorhombic 10.1088/0953-8984/19/10/106218 10.1107/S0567740877004415 PMC-009 L-Histidine Hydrochloride Monohydrate C6H10N3O2+·Cl−·H2O Organic Hydrogen-Bonded Molecular Salt Crystal P2₁2₁2₁ orthorhombic 10.1088/0953-8984/19/10/106218 10.1107/S0567740877004415 1176648 CAS 5934-29-2 HISTCM12 L-Histidine Hydrochloride Monohydrate L-Histidine monohydrochloride monohydrate L-Histidine·HCl·H₂O L-Histidinium chloride monohydrate PMC-009 piezoelectric L-Histidine hydrochloride monohydrate is an organic molecular salt (histidinium cation + chloride anion + water) that crystallizes in the non-centrosymmetric orthorhombic space group P2₁2₁2₁ (point group 222). It is piezoelectric: synchrotron X-ray multiple diffraction (Renninger scanning) measured its three symmetry-allowed shear coefficients d14 = 2.25(9)×10⁻¹⁰, d25 = 4.1(5)×10⁻¹¹ and d36 = 2.3(2)×10⁻¹⁰ C/N.

Benzimidazole Benzimidazole Benzimidazole Benzimidazole Benzimidazole 1,3-benzodiazole 1118210 1H-benzimidazole BMZ BZDMAZ01 Benzimidazole DrugBank: DB02962 PMC-011 benzoglyoxaline o-phenyleneformamidine 1,3-benzodiazole 1118210 1H-benzimidazole BMZ BZDMAZ01 Benzimidazole DrugBank: DB02962 PMC-011 benzoglyoxaline o-phenyleneformamidine 1,3-benzodiazole 1118210 1H-benzimidazole BMZ BZDMAZ01 Benzimidazole DrugBank: DB02962 PMC-011 benzoglyoxaline o-phenyleneformamidine PMC-011 C7H6N2 BZDMAZ01 1118210 Pna2₁ orthorhombic 10.1007/s00339-012-7448-7 10.1107/S0567740874005528 PMC-011 Benzimidazole C7H6N2 Organic Hydrogen-Bonded Molecular Crystal Pna2₁ orthorhombic 10.1007/s00339-012-7448-7 10.1107/S0567740874005528 1,3-benzodiazole 1118210 1H-benzimidazole BMZ BZDMAZ01 Benzimidazole DrugBank: DB02962 PMC-011 benzoglyoxaline o-phenyleneformamidine piezoelectric Benzimidazole (BMZ) is an organic hydrogen-bonded molecular crystal that crystallizes in the non-centrosymmetric, polar orthorhombic space group Pna2₁ (point group mm2). It is a nonlinear optical material (SHG ~4.5× KDP) and is piezoelectric: a PM200 piezometer measured d33 = 0.1 pC/N and d31 = 1.85 pC/N.

Imidazolium D-camphorsulfonate Imidazolium D-camphorsulfonate Imidazolium D-camphorsulfonate Imidazolium D-camphorsulfonate Imidazolium D-camphorsulfonate 2083426 D-ImCS Imidazolium D-camphorsulfonate JANCAG PMC-012 imidazolium D-camphorsulfonate 2083426 D-ImCS Imidazolium D-camphorsulfonate JANCAG PMC-012 imidazolium D-camphorsulfonate 2083426 D-ImCS Imidazolium D-camphorsulfonate JANCAG PMC-012 imidazolium D-camphorsulfonate PMC-012 C13H20N2O4S JANCAG 2083426 P2₁ monoclinic 10.1021/acs.chemmater.1c01663 10.1021/acs.chemmater.1c01663 PMC-012 Imidazolium D-camphorsulfonate C13H20N2O4S Organic Molecular Ionic Crystal P2₁ monoclinic 10.1021/acs.chemmater.1c01663 10.1021/acs.chemmater.1c01663 2083426 D-ImCS Imidazolium D-camphorsulfonate JANCAG PMC-012 imidazolium D-camphorsulfonate piezoelectric ferroelectric pyroelectric Imidazolium D-camphorsulfonate (D-ImCS) is a metal-free organic molecular ionic crystal: an imidazolium cation paired with a homochiral D-camphorsulfonate anion, linked by N–H···O hydrogen-bonded chains. It crystallizes in the chiral-polar monoclinic space group P2₁ (point group 2) at 173 K and is a molecular ferroelectric, undergoing a 222F2-type ferroelectric phase transition at Tc = 370 K. Ferroelectricity is confirmed by PFM domain switching and hysteresis loops; the quasi-static (Berlincourt) piezoelectric coefficient along the polar [010] axis is ±20 pC/N, comparable to triglycine sulfate.

Imidazolium L-camphorsulfonate Imidazolium L-camphorsulfonate Imidazolium L-camphorsulfonate Imidazolium L-camphorsulfonate Imidazolium L-camphorsulfonate 1H-imidazol-3-ium L-camphorsulfonate 2083425 Imidazolium L-camphorsulfonate L-ImCS PMC-013 PONQEP01 imidazolium L-camphorsulfonate 1H-imidazol-3-ium L-camphorsulfonate 2083425 Imidazolium L-camphorsulfonate L-ImCS PMC-013 PONQEP01 imidazolium L-camphorsulfonate 1H-imidazol-3-ium L-camphorsulfonate 2083425 Imidazolium L-camphorsulfonate L-ImCS PMC-013 PONQEP01 imidazolium L-camphorsulfonate PMC-013 C13H20N2O4S PONQEP01 2083425 P2₁ monoclinic 10.1021/acs.chemmater.1c01663 10.1021/acs.chemmater.1c01663 PMC-013 Imidazolium L-camphorsulfonate C13H20N2O4S Organic Molecular Ionic Crystal P2₁ monoclinic 10.1021/acs.chemmater.1c01663 10.1021/acs.chemmater.1c01663 1H-imidazol-3-ium L-camphorsulfonate 2083425 Imidazolium L-camphorsulfonate L-ImCS PMC-013 PONQEP01 imidazolium L-camphorsulfonate piezoelectric ferroelectric pyroelectric Imidazolium L-camphorsulfonate (L-ImCS) is a metal-free organic molecular ionic crystal: an imidazolium cation paired with a homochiral L-camphorsulfonate anion, linked by N–H···O hydrogen-bonded chains. It crystallizes in the chiral-polar monoclinic space group P2₁ (point group 2) at 173 K and is a molecular ferroelectric, undergoing a 222F2-type ferroelectric phase transition at Tc = 367 K. Ferroelectricity is confirmed directly on L-ImCS by PFM domain imaging, polarization switching, and hysteresis loops; the quasi-static (Berlincourt) piezoelectric coefficient along the polar [010] axis is ±19 pC/N (local PFM: 15.8 pC/N), comparable to triglycine sulfate. L-ImCS is the enantiomer of D-ImCS (PMC-012).

Triglycine Sulfate (TGS) Triglycine Sulfate (TGS) Triglycine Sulfate (TGS) Triglycine Sulfate (TGS) Triglycine Sulfate (TGS) 2144163 PMC-015 TGLYSU32 TGS Triglycine Sulfate (TGS) bis(glycinium) sulfate–glycine (1/1) triglycine sulphate tris(glycine) sulfuric acid 2144163 PMC-015 TGLYSU32 TGS Triglycine Sulfate (TGS) bis(glycinium) sulfate–glycine (1/1) triglycine sulphate tris(glycine) sulfuric acid 2144163 PMC-015 TGLYSU32 TGS Triglycine Sulfate (TGS) bis(glycinium) sulfate–glycine (1/1) triglycine sulphate tris(glycine) sulfuric acid PMC-015 (C2H5NO2)3·H2SO4 TGLYSU32 2144163 P2₁ monoclinic 10.1143/JJAP.1.13 10.1107/S2056989022000858 PMC-015 Triglycine Sulfate (TGS) (C2H5NO2)3·H2SO4 Organic Hydrogen-Bonded Molecular Salt Crystal P2₁ monoclinic 10.1143/JJAP.1.13 10.1107/S2056989022000858 2144163 PMC-015 TGLYSU32 TGS Triglycine Sulfate (TGS) bis(glycinium) sulfate–glycine (1/1) triglycine sulphate tris(glycine) sulfuric acid piezoelectric ferroelectric pyroelectric Triglycine sulfate (TGS) is the archetypal organic hydrogen-bonded molecular ferroelectric: two glycinium cations, one neutral glycine, and one sulfate anion linked by N–H···O and O–H···O hydrogen bonds. Below its Curie point (≈47.5 °C ≈ 321 K) it adopts the polar monoclinic space group P2₁ (point group 2) and is ferroelectric, pyroelectric, and piezoelectric; above Tc it transforms to the centrosymmetric paraelectric P2₁/m phase (point group 2/m), which is non-piezoelectric. The room-temperature (298 K) ferroelectric structure here was determined by time-of-flight neutron diffraction (Terasawa et al., 2022); the piezoelectric constants were measured by Ikeda, Tanaka & Toyoda (1962).
